input.topic: linear equations with unknowns on both sides

output.lesson_title: Solving Equations With x on Both Sides
output.objective: Solve linear equations where the variable appears on both sides by moving terms with inverse operations.

segments.narration: Today we solve equations where the letter appears on both sides of the equals sign.
segments.subtitle: Unknowns on Both Sides

visual.title: Linear Equations
visual.subtitle: Unknowns on Both Sides
visual.objective_text: Get all x-terms to one side using inverse operations.

segments.narration: Think of the equation as scales. Whatever we do to one side, we must do to the other.
segments.subtitle: Balance both sides

operations.narration_note: Take 2x from both sides to gather x-terms.
Take 3 from both sides to isolate x.

segments.narration: Start with 5x plus 3 equals 2x plus 15. Subtract 2x, then 3, then divide by 3.
segments.subtitle: Solve 5x + 3 = 2x + 15

segments.narration: Try 7x minus 4 equals 3x plus 16. Follow the same three steps.
segments.subtitle: Worked example: 7x − 4 = 3x + 16

segments.narration: A common mistake is only changing one side when you move a term. Both sides must stay balanced.
segments.subtitle: Operate on BOTH sides

mistaken_approach.statement: Subtracting 2x from only the left side.

visual.why_students_make_it: They think of one side as the 'answer side' and only operate on the other.

segments.narration: Recap: collect x-terms on one side, numbers on the other, keep both sides balanced, divide last.
segments.subtitle: Collect, balance, divide

key_points: Move x-terms to one side using inverse operations.
Move number terms to the other side.
Keep both sides balanced at every step.
visual.closing_statement: You can now solve linear equations with x on both sides.

branch_templates.example_question: Why subtract 2x from both sides first?
branch_templates.branch_narration_template: We subtract 2x so all the x-terms end up on one side, making the equation simpler to solve.
Here is another worked example following the same steps.
